goal: Activate the routed model topology for CQL's own development (max-intelligence variant, enforced cross-family review), add persistent-worker (sidekick) guidance with honest delegation-ledger semantics, and make the control plane truthful for persistent workers (many-rounds-to-one-session joins), worktree sessions, and the cost-per-accepted-record doctrine metric

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Hybrid dogfood config validates with reviewer heterogeneity verified cross-family (claude vs gpt), no drift
Follow-up delegation rounds to one worker session are linked as rounds with tokens attributed exactly once (no double-count, no unmatched flag)
Sessions started in git worktrees of this repo attribute to this repo's control-plane index in all three adapters
Loop metrics expose spend per accepted completion record (query, never a gate)
Hook suite green (stop gate, guard, sessionstart)
Routing suite green (variant pins hold with activated dogfood config)

constraints: Shipped consumer templates stay model-neutral: if root .claude/agents/*.md are what the installer/npm package ships, concrete model pins must not leak to consumers (verify manifest before committing frontmatter)
Control plane stays a query, never a gate; local-only; stdlib-only; no new dependencies
No token double-counting: a session's tokens are attributed at most once across delegation rows
Minimal diff; existing conventions; evals move with behavior (new control-plane behavior gets pinned cases)
Doc-count lint and derived eval counts stay consistent (canonical_gate_cases)
non_goals: Changing the user's default main-session model (stays Fable 5; balanced variant documented as the alternative)
Enabling control_plane in the committed dogfood config (stays opt-in per doctrine)
Executor-delegation topology change (implementer stays the main session; bench-first follow-up)
Re-adding any advisor pattern; tmux orchestration bridge; dashboard redesign
assumptions: Codex CLI is available for the cross-family review leg; if the pinned model id is unavailable the CLI's default model is used and recorded honestly
max-intelligence variant (as_of 2026-07-12, <90d) reflects the operator's real topology: Fable 5 main session, Sonnet 5 mapper, GPT-5.6 Sol review
verification_plan: re-execute all recorded pass commands via the verify umbrella (QUALITY_LOOP_TIMEOUT=300)
fresh-context independent review + cross-family Codex review + security review at the shell/network boundary
CI: validate + evals + npm-smoke on the PR branch

validation_contract.goal: The routed topology is active and enforced for CQL's own development; persistent-worker and worktree workflows are honestly accounted in the control plane; the doctrine cost metric is queryable.
evidence: check-config: config ok + reviewer heterogeneity verified (claude vs gpt)
seven eval suites green (251 core + 40 control add-on) incl. new/updated pinned cases and the release-version parity gate
npm package tests 34/34 from repo root (incl. packed-artifact neutrality)
installer copy is model-neutral (hook case: source pinned, installed copy inherit)

independent_review.notes: Cross-family review via codex exec -s read-only. Rounds 1-2 request_changes (8 blocking + 1 advisory applied; 1 preamble claim rebutted with evidence); rounds 5-10 surfaced 7 further blocking count/edge findings, each fixed and pinned; rounds 4 and 11 approve with no findings.

security_review.notes: Boundary: git subprocess in the indexer, localhost API surface, installer rewrite. Rounds 1-2 found canonical-containment bypass, trusted exact-slug attribution, NUL ValueError abort — all fixed fail-closed and pinned; round 3 approve with no findings.

completion_record.goal: Activate the routed model topology for CQL's own development (max-intelligence variant, enforced cross-family review), add persistent-worker (sidekick) guidance with honest delegation-ledger semantics, and make the control plane truthful for persistent workers (many-rounds-to-one-session joins), worktree sessions, and the cost-per-accepted-record doctrine metric
done_when: Hybrid dogfood config validates with reviewer heterogeneity verified cross-family (claude vs gpt), no drift
Follow-up delegation rounds to one worker session are linked as rounds with tokens attributed exactly once (no double-count, no unmatched flag)
Sessions started in git worktrees of this repo attribute to this repo's control-plane index in all three adapters
Loop metrics expose spend per accepted completion record (query, never a gate)
Hook suite green (stop gate, guard, sessionstart)
Routing suite green (variant pins hold with activated dogfood config)
evidence: python3 scripts/quality_loop.py check-config quality-loop.config.json -> pass (config ok; reviewer heterogeneity: verified (claude vs gpt))
python3 -m py_compile scripts/*.py evals/*.py hosts/claude-code/*.py -> pass (byte-compile clean)
python3 evals/run_evals.py -> pass (63/63 eval cases passed; derived counts 251 core + 40 add-on consistent)
python3 evals/run_control_evals.py -> pass (40/40 incl. follow-up rounds, worktree attribution, spend-per-accepted-record)
python3 evals/run_hook_evals.py -> pass (56/56 incl. installer neutralizes agent model pins)
python3 evals/run_routing_evals.py -> pass (30/30 routing cases; variant pins hold)
python3 scripts/quality_loop.py eval-cases evals/cases --config assets/quality-loop.config.example.json -> pass (19/19 static cases)
node --test packages/npm/test/*.test.mjs -> pass (34 pass, 0 fail (incl. prepack ships model-neutral templates))
completion_record.right_size: minimal_new_code (control-plane component); routing activation is reuse of shipped assets; docs-only guidance
risks: review-driven behavior change: every transcript must now prove its cwd — legitimately cwd-less transcripts (none observed in real hosts) would stop attributing
codex adapter widened from exact-root to descendant containment: subdir codex sessions now index (intended, disclosed in CHANGELOG)
declared main_session model matches the operator's real setup today; brief warns if the menu goes stale (>90d as_of)
completion_record.rollback: revert the release PR (single squash commit on main); npm: publish previous tag or deprecate 6.5.0; setup-models re-run restores agent frontmatter from any config state
follow_ups: existence lint for the config $schema pointer (advisory from review round 1)
per-round token slicing for persistent-worker sessions (ts-window split) if field use demands it
bench cell: executor-delegation topology (implementer as persistent Sonnet worker) per bench/PROTOCOL before any default change
bench cell: effort ceiling high vs xhigh for the REVIEW leg (repo doctrine vs recorded v6.0.1 xhigh yield)

harness_update: Two durable gates from this task's repeated mistakes: case_release_version_parity (npm == SKILL == README badge == gh example — version pins drifted twice) and the CHANGELOG historical-arithmetic check (global seds rewrote history twice this session; the lint now verifies old entries against their own stated totals).

callers_checked: delegations_with_sessions consumers: /api/delegations handler, task_timeline (per-task spend), dashboard Delegations view
_file_in_root callers: transcript indexing loop (quality_loop_control.py:1213-1215); codex adapter cwd gate (:805-806); droid _under_root (:873-877)
drift check reads agent-file frontmatter vs config (quality_loop_routing.py:1162-1189) — config and frontmatter must move together
count lint: evals/run_evals.py canonical_gate_cases()/control_addon_cases() checks doc count lines
tests: evals/run_control_evals.py (37 add-on cases; case_delegation_direct_session_id pins current duplicate flag; case conventions: case_* -> (bool, msg) in tmp dirs)
evals/run_evals.py + run_hook_evals.py + run_memory_evals.py + run_reality_evals.py + run_routing_evals.py (249 core)
patterns_to_follow: query-time joins, nothing stored (control plane doctrine)
graceful degradation on missing git (hooklib lesson from v6.4.0)
additive API fields; dashboard stays single-file self-contained (eval-pinned)
atomic frontmatter rewrite via setup-models, never hand-edited

minimality_decision.reason: Routing activation is pure reuse of shipped assets (rung 3: paste assets/routing/max-intelligence.json, run existing setup-models) and the sidekick guidance is docs-only; the rung-8 component is the control plane, where no lower rung suffices: no-change leaves the index misreporting exactly the workflows this release enables (follow-up rounds flagged duplicate_session_id/unmatched; worktree sessions invisible; doctrine KPI unqueryable); nothing exists to delete or reuse that expresses many-rounds-to-one-session or worktree membership; stdlib-only is preserved (subprocess git + sqlite, no new dependency); not expressible as a one-liner because the membership semantics span three adapters plus the join and must each stay behavior-identical for non-worktree paths.

plan: S1 activate routing: model_routing (max-intelligence) into quality-loop.config.json; setup-models rewrites mapper/planner frontmatter (committed — the repo's own truth); install.py neutralizes agent frontmatter to model: inherit (effort dropped) at its single copy choke point (install.py:230 loop) so consumers keep host-neutral templates + eval case pins it; codex leg stays print-only (reviewer model passed per-invocation via codex exec -m, never written to the user's global config); check-config + brief prove heterogeneity verified and no drift

S2 sidekick guidance: references/agentic-orchestration.md persistent-worker subsection (implementer may persist across fix rounds; reviewers never persist; ledger: one row per round, same session_id); docs/control-plane.md ledger semantics; one clause in SKILL.md Orchestrator Layer

S3 control plane: (a) delegations_with_sessions: later rows naming a claimed session become linked follow_up rounds, session attached without tokens (single-count preserved); (b) worktree-aware repo membership: git worktree list --porcelain roots (cached, graceful without git), applied to claude transcript_dirs/_file_in_root, codex exact-match, droid prefix-match, each preserving its current semantics per root; (c) loop_metrics: spend per accepted completion record (records with approving independent review joined to their task's linked sessions) + dashboard Metrics table
S4 evals: update case_delegation_direct_session_id; add follow-up-rounds case, worktree-scoping case, accepted-record-cost case; let count lint drive doc count updates

S5 release: CHANGELOG 6.5.0, SKILL.md + npm version bumps, verify umbrella, fresh + cross-family (codex) + security review, attest, PR, merge, tag+release, npm publish via CI, archive record, teardown

commands_run.evidence: config ok; reviewer heterogeneity: verified (claude vs gpt)
63/63 eval cases passed; derived counts 251 core + 40 add-on consistent
40/40 incl. follow-up rounds, worktree attribution, spend-per-accepted-record
56/56 incl. installer neutralizes agent model pins
34 pass, 0 fail (incl. prepack ships model-neutral templates)

review_findings.text: prepack copies .claude verbatim so the tarball ships pinned agent files despite install-time neutralization
review_findings.resolution: prepack.mjs neutralizeAgentPins strips model:/effort: in dist copies; npm test pins the packed artifact (packages/npm/test/cli.integration.test.mjs)

review_findings.text: delegation rows ordered lexicographically by artifact key: row #10 precedes #9, first-claim lands on the wrong round
review_findings.resolution: delegations_with_sessions re-sorts by (source_path, numeric key tail); follow-up-rounds case extended to 11 rounds asserting round 1 carries tokens

review_findings.text: spend-per-accepted-record let a live approval outrank an archived rejection (twin rule not applied)
review_findings.resolution: records canonicalized per task with archives winning both directions before verdict evaluation; disagreeing-twin cases pinned in both directions

review_findings.text: codex worktree attribution exact-root only (subdir cwds excluded) and a stale 60s roots cache could consume a new worktree's rollout permanently (offset at tail)
review_findings.resolution: codex adapter uses descendant containment via _under_root (raw + resolved cwd); repo_roots refreshed each index pass; roots-change deletes codex rollout file_state so consumed rollouts resurrect; pinned by late-worktree recovery scenario

review_findings.text: evals/README add-on table row still said 37; count lint only recognized the 'N add-on cases' phrase
review_findings.resolution: row updated to 40; lint gains _ADDON_ROW table-cell check

review_findings.text: $schema pointed at ./quality-loop.config.schema.json which does not exist at repo root
review_findings.resolution: pointer fixed to assets/quality-loop.config.schema.json; existence lint noted as follow-up

review_findings.text: reviewer preamble claimed a non-dict model_routing takes the gate-only path silently
review_findings.resolution: rebutted with evidence: probe config with model_routing:'garbage' exits 1 with full-validation errors (orchestration-present keys include model_routing regardless of type)

review_findings.text: round 2: README badge and shipped GitHub example still pinned v6.4.0 while SKILL/npm declare 6.5.0
review_findings.resolution: both bumped; case_release_version_parity added (npm == SKILL == README badge == gh example) — version drift is a repeated mistake, now a gate

review_findings.text: round 2: spend-per-accepted-record priced cache_creation tokens but did not display them (tokens summed 60, cost charged 100)
review_findings.resolution: cache_creation_tokens added to the metric, dashboard column, and the priced eval assertion with nonzero cache fields

review_findings.text: round 2: recorded npm evidence said 33 tests after the suite grew to 34
review_findings.resolution: evidence and validation contract updated to 34/34; suite re-run

review_findings.text: round 3: global seds corrupted historical CHANGELOG suite counts (v6.4.0 breakdown no longer summed to its stated total)
review_findings.resolution: history restored from origin/main; 6.5.0 section re-inserted; count lint now checks each historical entry's addends sum to its own stated total (RED probe replaying the sed is caught)

review_findings.text: security: _under_root raw-string prefix accepted repo/../foreign (canonicalization bypass exposing foreign session titles to the local API)
review_findings.resolution: canonical containment only — both sides resolved, is_relative_to, fail closed on unresolvable cwds; escape probe pinned

review_findings.text: security: exact slug dirs trusted without cwd proof; lossy slug flattening lets a colliding foreign project's transcripts ingest
review_findings.resolution: exact dirs now claim their root and every transcript passes the per-file cwd check or stays unattributed; foreign-cwd and cwd-less fixtures pinned

review_findings.text: security round 2: a JSON cwd containing an embedded NUL reached Path.resolve() and raised uncaught ValueError, aborting control-index (availability; API 500 instead of fail-closed)
review_findings.resolution: _safe_resolve and _file_in_root catch (OSError, ValueError); NUL probe + NUL-cwd transcript fixture pinned

review_findings.text: round 5: a hard-wrapped '249 / offline gate cases' pair in launch-kit escaped the per-line count lint, and recorded run_evals evidence went stale (62/62, 250) after the parity case landed
review_findings.resolution: doc fixed to 251; plain-count lint scans a whitespace-flattened view with a windowed historical exemption; evidence refreshed to 63/63, 251 and the suite re-run

review_findings.text: round 6: Behavioral suite table row still 62 (parenthetical dodged the sed; lint blind to per-suite rows) and the fixed 80-char historical window could mask an adjacent stale current count
review_findings.resolution: row fixed to 63; lint validates every runner-anchored suite row and scopes the historical exemption to the sentence clause; four self-test regressions added

review_findings.text: round 7: _clause split only on period-space; semicolon/em-dash boundaries still let a historical clause mask an adjacent stale count
review_findings.resolution: boundary set extended to [.;]\s|em-dash (colon/comma stay glue for the legit historical form); both probes self-tested

review_findings.text: round 8: legacy v6.4 schema-8 caches (consumed-foreign rollout offsets, no meta.repo_roots row) never reset, permanently omitting pre-upgrade worktree codex sessions
review_findings.resolution: absence of the meta row triggers the same codex offset reset as a roots change (no-op on fresh DBs); legacy-cache upgrade regression pinned

review_findings.text: round 9: per-dir single-winner root claims excluded one of two legitimate worktrees whose paths flatten to the same slug
review_findings.resolution: each dir carries its full candidate-root list; every transcript attributes by its own cwd against any candidate; two-worktree collision regression pinned

review_findings.text: round 10: symlink-loop cwds raised uncaught RuntimeError on Python 3.11 resolve(), aborting indexing
review_findings.resolution: _safe_resolve catches RuntimeError; symlink-loop probe pinned (foreign loops fail closed on every supported Python)
